title: malva lusitanica subsp. pallescens
intro: Malva lusitanica subsp. pallescens is a subspecies of the Malva lusitanica plant, commonly known as the white mallow or pale mallow, which is a member of the mallow family (Malvaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Malva lusitanica subsp. pallescens include reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and salves, to treat skin conditions and other ailments.
